sources.text:
Privacy manifest
Note: If your Algolia implementation collects tracking data, Apple requires you to add a privacy manifest that describes your reason and method for collecting tracking data.
 
What is a privacy manifest?
A privacy manifest is a file in your Xcode project that describes the reason your app and third-party SDKs collect data, along with their data-collection methods. Each of your third-party SDKs that track data require their own privacy manifest. When you create your app’s privacy report, these privacy manifest files are automatically aggregated into a single report.
 
Declaring your privacy manifest
Step 1: Review current policies
Before you begin, review your Algolia SDK’s current data collection policies and API usage within your application. Determine which elements of your application collect data and what APIs you are using.
 
See the following pages for more documentation about the collection of end-user data by Algolia’s SDK’s. Please note that most of the time, end-user data collection is needed for Algolia features to operate (e.g. Recommend, Re-Ranking, Personalisation, Neuralsearch).

User token
Event types
Get started with click and conversion events

 
Step 2: Declare data collection practices
We have provided template privacy manifests for both the InstantSearch iOS Library and the Swift API Client. You can start with these templates to ensure you cover all required aspects.
By default, the Algolia SDK does not collect tracking data. However, depending on your Algolia SDK configuration, you may be required to list Algolia-specific data in your app’s privacy manifest.
 
Step 3: Customize privacy manifest
Edit the provided files to reflect the specific data collection and API usage of your applications. Ensure each entry is clearly justified and aligns with Apple’s guidelines.
 
Step 4: Integrate privacy manifest into application
Once your privacy manifest is customized and complete, integrate into your iOS project. This involves adding your files to your project settings and ensuring it is included in your build process.
sources.label: https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/articles/24623000531217-Privacy-manifest

sources.text:
How can I allow specific stop words in Algolia?
If you want certain words—such as "the", "what", "that", or "it" to be treated as regular searchable terms (instead of being ignored as stop words), you can update your Words Ignored dictionary in the Algolia Dashboard.Steps to allow specific stop words
In the Algolia Dashboard, select your Search product.
In the left-hand menu, go to Dictionaries.
In the top-right corner, select the language used by your index.
Open the Words Ignored dictionary.
Search for the word you want to allow (e.g. "the").
Then, depending on the word's origin:
If it is an Algolia-provided stop word, click Disable to stop Algolia from removing it from queries.
If it is a custom stop word you previously added, you can either Disable it or Remove it entirely.
Click Review and Save to apply your changes.Once disabled or removed, the word will be treated like any other searchable term—as long as stop word removal (removeStopWords) is still active for that language.Optional: Disable stop word removal entirelyIf you don’t want any stop words removed from queries, you can disable the feature completely by setting the removeStopWords parameter to false for your index.NeuralSearch compatibilityPlease note: Custom stop word dictionaries are not currently supported with NeuralSearch. If you're using NeuralSearch, modifications made through custom dictionaries will not be applied.Resources
Customize stop words
removeStopWords parameter
How can I make sure Algolia NeuralSearch is working correctly?
sources.label: https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/articles/15805710783889-How-can-I-allow-specific-stop-words-in-Algolia

sources.text:
What impacts build time?
Please note that we do not provide an SLA/SLO on indexing. We do not guarantee a build time.If you are on a shared cluster: the activities of other users can impact your build time. Several users sending peak indexing jobs can slow down all other users. In this case, dedicated infrastructure can improve your build time.Regardless of whether you are on a shared or dedicated infrastructure, the way the jobs are scheduled and the way the batches are merged affects build time.The following can also impact how long it takes for indexing to complete:

Large indices have an impact on the build time.

Replicas impact build time: they may need several indices to be built or rebuilt, which can lead to a slow down in the indexing process. Standard replicas are the most impactful for build times.As an example, if we build N indices at most at once, and if you have N replicas, it means that we’ll build an index alone after building the N first indices, leading to double the build time. Thus, the building of other indices can be stopped until the big index is processed. Limiting the number of replicas can help.


Large batches can also take time to be absorbed. Splitting large batches into small batches can reduce the “stuck time”, but overall will be less efficient.

More searchable attributes mean longer build time
More attributes for faceting mean longer build time
In addition, note that build time is affected when using the NeuralSearch feature. This is because the NeuralSearch feature uses a different infrastructure, and will require building a second index that is used by the feature.
sources.label: https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/articles/5968047650321-What-impacts-build-time

sources.text:
How can I make sure Algolia NeuralSearch is working correctly?
You can review our documentation on getting started with NeuralSearch.Broadly, you'll want to ensure that:

You are on an eligible plan (either the Elevate or Elevate Ecomm plan).
You have enabled NeuralSearch on your index as per this guide.
You've configured your searchable attributes as per this guide and your relevance as per this guide. This ensures that queries not yet generating a semantic score will still generate a relevant keyword score.  
The data structure of your record is a good use case for NeuralSearch (see "Data Structure" section below).
You are sending events (see "Events" section below).
Your use case doesn't conflict with any of the known limitations (see "Limitations" section below).

 
Data Structure
Here's an example of a record structure and settings that would work with NeuralSearch:

 
 
NeuralSearch is not the best solution for search implementations that only search on SKUs or other exact matches.
 
Events

Have you implemented events?
Are your events meeting the prerequisite requirements for NeuralSearch? The requirements for the number of events may change, but the latest information can be found in this documentation. 
Are your events valid?

Use the Events Debugger to assess whether or not your events are valid. Valid events will have a green dot in the "Status" column as shown below:




You should also make sure that you aren't sending bot-generated events. For more information, please refer to our guide on how to deal with bots.
 
Limitations 
NeuralSearch currently has some feature differences and limitations, including:

Multi-region DSN is not currently supported.
Index size is limited to 100 GB.
Application size is limited to 1 TB.
You cannot use the deleteBy operation. Use the delete method instead, which is significantly more performant.

Custom dictionaries are not currently available.
Real time logs are not currently available.

Vault is not currently available
There are some internal indexing differences.
NeuralSearch is not configurable for HIPAA-Enabled Service Features
sources.label: https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/articles/15032552758161-How-can-I-make-sure-Algolia-NeuralSearch-is-working-correctly

answer.answer:
Algolia NeuralSearch is an AI-powered feature that improves search relevance by understanding the underlying meaning and intent of a query, not just matching keywords. It works in tandem with Algolia's core keyword search to deliver more accurate results, especially for complex, natural language queries.

### How it works

NeuralSearch uses a technology called [semantic search](https://www.algolia.com/blog/product/semantic-search-how-it-works-who-its-for/), which goes beyond keyword matching to comprehend the concepts in a query. It's trained on your own click and conversion data to learn the associations between queries and the most relevant results.

Instead of replacing the standard ranking algorithm, NeuralSearch enhances it. The ranking process combines scores from both keyword search and semantic search to produce a final, more relevant ranking.

### How results are ranked

According to the support article "[How does NeuralSearch rank results?](https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/articles/17700562878481-How-does-NeuralSearch-rank-results-)", the final ranking is determined by a `neural score` which is a combination of two separate scores:

*   **Keyword Score**: This is the traditional relevance score produced by Algolia's tie-breaking algorithm based on how well a record matches the query's keywords.
*   **Semantic Score**: This score represents how well a record matches the query from a conceptual, vector-based search. It's based on what's called "neural hash similarity."

The `neural score` combines both the keyword and semantic scores to measure how relevant a record is compared to the other results for that specific query. This hybrid approach ensures the precision of keyword search is complemented by the conceptual understanding of AI.

For NeuralSearch to work effectively, it must be enabled on your index and you must be sending click and conversion events, as these are used to train the model. It is most effective for businesses that deal with natural language queries and is not the best solution for implementations that only search on exact matches like SKUs.

Are you looking to improve results for longer, more descriptive user queries?

sources.text:
How does Algolia rank results on empty queries?
Your current Ranking and Sorting Configuration affects how your results displayed for empty queries.
If there are no custom ranking attributes to break ties between the records with empty queries, your records are ranked by their objectIDs.

 
You can also check how your ranking algorithm ordered your search results by hovering the badge shaped icon in the bottom of the each record.
If you'd like to customize the search results on empty queries, you may use a replica. A replica is a copy of an index, which you can use different sorting strategies for the same data. For more information, please review our Search in a replica index documentation.
sources.label: https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/articles/10524034357393-How-does-Algolia-rank-results-on-empty-queries

sources.text:
What happens if we set analytics as false for a query but set clickAnalytics as true? Will click analytics still work and set a queryID for searches so I can send events?
Yes, the analytics and click analytics search parameters are separate.If you set analytics as false (which means that the search will not appear in your analytics) you can still set clickAnalytics as true, and use the search to send events.However this click event will not be tracked in your analytics, because there will be no search in your analytics to link it back to. As there will be no search information, the events can be used for Algolia Recommend or Personalization but they will not work with NeuralSearch, Dynamic Re-Ranking, Query Categorisation or A/B Testing which all require events to be linked to a search.

questions.prompt: What is vector search?

answer.answer: [WEBSITE STUB] No live algolia.com Ask-AI capture wired yet for: "What is vector search?". This placeholder exists so the harness runs end-to-end; lab/capture will replace it.

Vector search is a method that uses machine learning to find the most conceptually similar items from a database, going beyond simple keyword matching. It achieves this by representing data—such as text, images, or audio—as numerical vectors, known as **vector embeddings** ([details and examples](https://www.algolia.com/blog/ai/what-is-vector-search/)).

### How Vector Search Works

Vector search involves a few key concepts:

*   **Vector Embeddings:** Data is converted into vectors, which are arrays of numbers. These vectors are generated by machine learning models, like neural networks, and are designed to capture the semantic meaning and context of the original data. For example, the vectors for "king" and "queen" would be located close to each other in a multi-dimensional space because they are conceptually similar ([read more on vectors](https://www.algolia.com/blog/ai/what-are-vectors-and-how-do-they-apply-to-machine-learning/)).
*   **Vector Database:** These specialized databases are designed to store and efficiently query these vector embeddings. They can quickly compare a query vector against millions of other vectors to find the closest matches.
*   **Similarity Search:** When you perform a search, your query is also converted into a vector. The system then searches the vector database for items whose vectors are closest to the query's vector. This "closeness" is a measure of semantic similarity, allowing the search to understand the intent behind the query, not just the specific words used.

### Benefits of Vector Search

This approach allows for a more intuitive and human-like search experience. For instance, you could search for "things to put on my feet in summer" and get results like "sandals" or "flip-flops," even if those exact words weren't in your query. It's particularly powerful for handling complex queries, synonyms, and even searching with images or other non-text data.

For more support or info, please feel free to browse our [support articles](https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/search) or [docs](https://www.algolia.com/doc). If you still need help, [submit a ticket](https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/requests/new) for customer support.

answer.answer:
Vector search is a method that finds results based on their semantic meaning and context, rather than just matching keywords. It uses machine learning to understand the intent behind a user's query.

### How it works
Vector search represents text as a series of numbers in a multi-dimensional space, known as "word vectors" or "embeddings." These vectors capture the semantic meaning of words, so that words with similar meanings are located close to each other.

When you search for a term, vector search looks for items with similar vectors, returning results that are semantically similar, even if they don't share the exact same keywords. For example, a search for "scent" could return results for "perfume" or "fragrance."

At Algolia, vector search is a key component of our [NeuralSearch](https://www.algolia.com/products/features/neuralsearch) capability, which combines it with traditional keyword search to deliver the most relevant results.

Are you working with unstructured data or trying to improve search for complex, natural language queries?

questions.prompt: What is Dynamic Re-Ranking and how does it work?

answer.answer: [WEBSITE STUB] No live algolia.com Ask-AI capture wired yet for: "What is Dynamic Re-Ranking and how does it work?". This placeholder exists so the harness runs end-to-end; lab/capture will replace it.

Dynamic Re-Ranking is an AI-powered feature that automatically boosts the most relevant and popular items in search results. It works by analyzing user interactions, such as clicks and conversions, to identify trends and improve the ranking of items that perform best.

### How it works
Dynamic Re-Ranking uses a machine learning model to optimize search results based on collective user behavior. The process is as follows:

1.  **Event Collection**: The feature relies on user interaction data. You must send **click and conversion events** to Algolia for the model to learn from ([details and examples](https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/articles/37490688595217-What-s-the-impact-of-changing-my-Algolia-index-name)).
2.  **AI Model Training**: Algolia's AI model analyzes these events to identify which items are most frequently clicked or purchased for specific queries across all users.
3.  **Automatic Re-Ranking**: Based on this analysis, the model automatically boosts the best-performing items, pushing them higher in the search results. This process is continuous, allowing the model to adapt to new trends and changes in user behavior.

This feature operates at the index level and requires a sufficient amount of event data to be collected before it can effectively optimize rankings. It complements other merchandising and relevance configurations by applying a final boost based on an item's overall popularity.

For more support or info, please feel free to browse our [support articles](https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/search) or [docs](https://www.algolia.com/doc). If you still need help, [submit a ticket](https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/requests/new) for customer support.

sources.text:
What’s the impact of changing my Algolia index name?
Changing the name of an Algolia index can have significant consequences on your Algolia implementation and some key features.The most important consideration is that any code, configuration, or integration referencing the original index name must be updated. Otherwise, this change can lead to unexpected behavior or application errors. When renaming an index the below warning message will appear. This relates to the impact on Access and Analytics. In addition to this, renaming will also have an impact on frontend integrations, events tracking, and any features dependent on index-specific events, as these are not transferred to the new index name.Full details on the various impacts can be found below:

Access:
Within Team Member permissions, it's possible to restrict which indices users have access to, for example, you may declare dev_*, staging_* to grant a user access to all indices with a common prefix. Therefore, if you change the name of an index that you have specified a user has access to, you will need to ensure you update the name in the user's index permission settings.


Frontend Integrations:
Any frontend code, integrations, or connectors that reference the old index name will need to be updated to use the new index name. Otherwise, this change can lead to unexpected behavior or application errors.


Analytics: 
Analytics data is tied to an index by its name, not by an internal identifier. Analytics events are collected and stored on a separate analytics server from the search indices, and the index name is used as the link between them. As a result, renaming an index breaks this association, and any historical analytics data collected under the old name will no longer be accessible under the new one.


Event Tracking: 
Events are tied to the specific index name. If you change the index name, you cannot transfer historical events to the new index. More information in this article: Can I transfer events to a different index?



Dynamic Re-Ranking: 

Algolia’s Dynamic Re-Ranking feature operates at the index level, which means it cannot draw upon insights from multiple indices simultaneously. After changing the index name, Dynamic Re-Ranking loses access to the historical event data that powers optimized ranking. As a result, the new index must wait until enough events have been collected before the feature will work again.
The original configuration for the index won't match anymore upon index renaming. Any configuration will need to be re-created for this feature.
When renaming an index, the enableReRanking index settings will be set to false.



Query Categorization (QCat): 

QCat uses historical queries and click data. Renaming the index breaks the link to the event data. The new index must wait until enough events have been collected before the feature will work again. 
The original configuration for the index won't match anymore upon index renaming. Any configuration will need to be re-created for this feature.



Dynamic Synonym Suggestions: 

Suggestions are based on search and click analytics. Renaming or copying an index breaks the connection to historical data.
The original configuration for the index won't match anymore upon index renaming. Any configuration will need to be re-created for this feature.
Every declined synonym will be lost and they will potentially be seen in "pending" again. Historically accepted synonyms will still exist on the renamed index, but they won't be visible in the DSS section of the dashboard



Query Suggestions:
If the index is set as the source index for a Query Suggestions index, the configuration for that QS index should be updated to reflect the new index name.


Neural Search:
Neural training data is index-specific. A renamed/copied index loses link to prior data.


Virtual Replicas (VRs):
If you copy an index to another app and rename it, VRs will also be copied and remain as VRs of their respective new primary indices. The relationship is preserved, but the names will reflect the new primary index. More information in this article: Do virtual replicas stay connected when I copy indices with new names to another app?
sources.label: https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/articles/37490688595217-What-s-the-impact-of-changing-my-Algolia-index-name

answer.answer:
Dynamic Re-Ranking automatically adjusts search rankings based on user behavior, such as clicks and conversions, to promote the most popular and relevant results.

### How it works

The core idea of [Dynamic Re-Ranking](https://www.algolia.com/doc/guides/algolia-ai/re-ranking/) is to detect trends in your users' behavior and use that data to improve the ranking of search results. Instead of relying only on a static ranking formula, this feature automatically re-ranks results to favor items that are getting the most engagement.

The goal is to improve key business metrics by:
*   **Boosting Conversions:** By analyzing user clicks and conversions, the system can automatically promote products that are currently popular, helping to increase sales ([Dynamic Re-Ranking: Boosting Conversions](https://academy.algolia.com/training/7022018c-5c91-11eb-bb84-06c0361096e5)).
*   **Increasing Revenue:** You can configure the feature to surface products that drive the most revenue, preventing high-converting but low-value items from dominating the top results ([Dynamic Re-Ranking for Revenue: Boosting Revenue](https://academy.algolia.com/training/019830c1-d08d-7620-bb92-c078630f90b7)).

This approach focuses on what's described as the "three R's" of search: Relevance, Ranking, and Revenue ([Masterclass: Dynamic Re-Ranking](https://academy.algolia.com/training/0192ba06-d55e-74ab-b717-520d5eee55ea)).

### Advanced features and availability

Dynamic Re-Ranking can also be enabled on category pages to promote popular products in a browsing context ([Enable Dynamic Re-Ranking on category pages](https://www.algolia.com/doc/guides/algolia-ai/re-ranking/tutorials/re-rank-category-pages/)). For more timely adjustments, an [Advanced Dynamic Re-Ranking](https://academy.algolia.com/training/01944673-3558-7be5-a2d8-528417b3e7e4) option offers hourly updates to popularity scores and the ability to re-rank even on empty queries.

Please note that this feature isn't available on every plan, so you may need to refer to your pricing plan to see if it’s included.

Are you looking to optimize for general popularity and conversions, or more specifically for business revenue?

questions.prompt: How does Algolia support ecommerce and retail search?

answer.answer: [WEBSITE STUB] No live algolia.com Ask-AI capture wired yet for: "How does Algolia support ecommerce and retail search?". This placeholder exists so the harness runs end-to-end; lab/capture will replace it.

Algolia provides a powerful suite of tools designed to help ecommerce and retail businesses create fast, relevant, and personalized search and discovery experiences. This enhances the customer experience and drives conversions.

### Key Features for Ecommerce and Retail

*   **Fast and Relevant Search:** Algolia's core strength is its speed and relevance, delivering instant search results that can handle complex queries and large product catalogs. This includes typo tolerance and faceting to help shoppers easily refine their searches by attributes like size, color, brand, or price.
*   **Merchandising and Promotion:** Business teams can use a visual editor to create merchandising rules, promote specific products, and manage campaigns without writing any code. This allows for strategic placement of items to boost visibility for new arrivals, bestsellers, or promotional goods.
*   **Personalization:** Algolia's AI can deliver personalized search results and recommendations for each user based on their behavior, such as past purchases, searches, and clicks. This creates a unique shopping experience that can increase user engagement and average order value.
*   **Semantic and Image Search:** Modern ecommerce often involves more than just keywords. Algolia supports **semantic search** to understand the user's intent behind a query and provides features like **image search**, which is increasingly popular on fashion and retail websites ([details and examples](https://www.algolia.com/doc/guides/building-search-ui/resources/ui-kit/vue/)).
*   **Sponsored Products and Retail Media:** For retailers with a marketplace or those who want to monetize their search results, Algolia provides strategies for integrating sponsored products seamlessly into the user experience ([details and examples](https://www.algolia.com/doc/guides/solutions/ecommerce/sponsored-products/guides/parallel/)).
*   **Analytics and A/B Testing:** Retailers can gain insights from rich analytics on top searches, queries with no results, and click patterns. This data allows for continuous optimization of the search experience through A/B testing to understand the impact of changes on key business metrics.

By leveraging these capabilities, ecommerce and retail businesses can build a search and discovery experience that meets the high expectations of modern online shoppers.

For more support or info, please feel free to browse our [support articles](https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/search) or [docs](https://www.algolia.com/doc). If you still need help, [submit a ticket](https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/requests/new) for customer support.

answer.answer:
Algolia's AI Search platform provides a comprehensive suite of tools for ecommerce and retail, designed to create fast, relevant, and personalized experiences that increase conversions and revenue. It moves beyond simple keyword matching to help customers discover products easily.

### AI-powered search and discovery
Algolia enhances the entire search and discovery journey. Core features that support ecommerce and retail businesses include:

*   **Merchandising tools**: You can use tools like the [Visual Editor](https://www.algolia.com/customers/END./) and [Rules](https://www.algolia.com/customers/DocMorris/) to create and manage merchandising strategies. This allows you to promote specific products, run seasonal campaigns, and control the search experience to align with business goals.
*   **Dynamic Re-ranking**: This feature automatically boosts the visibility of the best-performing products in search results, helping to maximize conversions.
*   **A/B Testing**: You can experiment with different search and relevance configurations to find what works best for your users and optimize your setup for key metrics like conversion rates.
*   **AI Synonyms**: This feature uses artificial intelligence to automatically detect and suggest relevant synonyms, improving the quality of search results even when customers use different terms for the same product.
*   **Analytics**: Algolia provides detailed analytics to help you understand user behavior, evaluate the performance of your search, and identify opportunities for improvement.

For example, the fashion retailer [Oh Polly](https://www.algolia.com/de/customers/bike-totaal/) used Algolia to improve its customer search experience and increase revenue. Similarly, [Arc'teryx](https://www.algolia.com/customers/Arcteryx/) was able to scale its product search and discovery capabilities, and [DocMorris](https://www.algolia.com/customers/DocMorris/) improved its results with AI Search.

Are you currently more focused on improving search relevance, or on giving your merchandising teams more control over the user experience?

sources.text:
Commercial Account Executive, DACH

At Algolia, we’re proud to be a pioneer and market leader in AI Search, empowering 17,000+ businesses to deliver blazing-fast, predictive search and browse experiences at internet scale. Every week, we power over 30 billion search requests — four times more than Microsoft Bing, Yahoo, Baidu, Yandex, and DuckDuckGo combined.

In 2021, we raised $150 million in Series D funding, quadrupling our valuation to $2.25 billion. This strong foundation enables us to keep investing in our market-leading platform and serving incredible customers like Under Armour, PetSmart, Stripe, Gymshark, and Walgreens.

At Algolia, we are passionate about helping businesses connect their end-users with what matters most in milliseconds!

 

As an Algolia Account Executive, you will be responsible for creating strong relationships with our customers/prospects and building on these relationships to expand our footprint or close new businesses. You should be comfortable researching prospective customers and adapting sales strategies to meet their needs. You will also play an integral role in defining our sales model. You know the ins and outs of Algolia, understand how we can benefit each prospect and will ultimately be the front line of our revenue growth in the German-speaking and CEMEA markets.

 

Algolia has been growing exponentially over the past few years with Germany representing one of our core markets. We are now expanding our existing German-speaking team to further accelerate in this region, providing you with the opportunity to become one of the core players in our success story. You will be an important voice in how we address this market and able to gain a lot of visibility both within our fast-growing tech company as well as within the German eCommerce/ retail segment.

 

Are you a resilient problem-solver who isn't afraid to think outside the box and get their hands dirty? We expect you to take ownership and be able to execute, defining your own territory strategy. We're looking for candidates who raise the level and bring sales excellence to the revenue organisation. You should value and practice transparency, have the humility to accept your weaknesses and continuously strive to improve both personally and professionally.

YOUR ROLE WILL CONSIST OF:

  • Managing and growing a sales pipeline
  • Driving inbound and outbound leads through complex sales cycles to closed won
  • Working your network and collaborating with external partners to source and facilitate new projects in the Commercial segment
  • Managing and prospecting with an existing account base to drive usage-based and feature-based upsells
  • Generating new projects from booking self-driven outbound meetings
  • Building compelling demonstrations with the pre-sales team, sales material and ROI studies bringing value to our customers
  • Forecasting accurately to help the team hit their numbers
  • Collaborating with sales developers, solutions engineers, customer success managers, legal, partner managers and marketing.

YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF YOU HAVE:

  • 2 - 4 years of experience in a closing role (esp. in B2B, MarTech, SaaS tech sales)
  • Excellent spoken and written German and English skills, Polish or Czech a plus
  • Hunter mindset: Strong familiarity with outbound prospecting techniques, tools and processes
  • Strong Plus: Experience in working with partners (solution integrators, consultancies, independent software vendors)
  • Multi-tasker who can adapt to changing situations
  • Quick thinker, must be able to handle questions on the fly

NICE TO HAVE:

  • You love tech sales;
  • You have a curious mindset and are capable to understand prospects’ pains and how a deep tech solution like Algolia can solve it;
  • You thrive in dynamic environments and are capable to adjust to constant change;
  • You are excited by the idea of building things and have a structured and strategic approach to working your territory;
  • You believe you are in charge of your own success and you have the grit to continue going when things get challenging.

#LI-Hybrid 

FLEXIBLE WORKPLACE STRATEGY:

Algolia’s flexible workplace model is designed to empower all Algolians to fulfill our mission to power search and discovery with ease. We place an emphasis on an individual’s impact, contribution, and output, over their physical location. Algolia is a high-trust environment and many of our team members have the autonomy to choose where they want to work and when. 

We have a global presence with offices in Paris, NYC, London, Sydney and Bucharest, however we also offer many of our team members the option to work remotely either as fully remote or hybrid-remote employees. Positions listed as "Remote" are only available for remote work within the specified country. Positions listed within a specific city are only available in that location - depending on the role it may be available with either a hybrid-remote or in-office schedule.

WE’RE LOOKING FOR SOMEONE WHO CAN LIVE OUR VALUES:

  • GRIT - Problem-solving and perseverance capability in an ever-changing and growing environment.
  • TRUST - Willingness to trust our co-workers and to take ownership.
  • CANDOR - Ability to receive and give constructive feedback.
  • CARE - Genuine care about other team members, our clients and the decisions we make in the company.
  • HUMILITY - Aptitude for learning from others, putting ego aside.

We’re looking for talented, passionate people to help build the world’s best search and discovery technology. We value autonomy, diversity, and collaboration. We’re committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone is respected and supported—regardless of race, age, ancestry, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, color, veteran status, disability, or socioeconomic background.

IMPORTANT NOTICE FOR CANDIDATES - Recruitment Fraud Notice

We’ve recently seen an increase in recruitment scams targeting job seekers. To help protect yourself, please keep the following in mind:

  • Our open positions may appear on third-party job boards, but the best way to apply safely is directly through our careers page.
  • All genuine communication from Algolia will come from an @algolia.com email address. If you receive an email from someone claiming to work at Algolia who does not have an @algolia.com email address, please do not respond or share any personal information.
  • We’ll never ask for payments, purchases, or financial details during the hiring process.

READY TO APPLY?

If you share our values and our enthusiasm for building the world’s best search & discovery technology, we’d love to review your application!
sources.label: https://job-boards.greenhouse.io/algolia/jobs/5854606004

  • Generating new projects from booking self-driven outbound meetings
  • Building compelling demonstrations with the pre-sales team, sales material and ROI studies bringing value to our customers
  • Forecasting accurately to help the team hit their numbers
  • Collaborating with sales developers, solutions engineers, customer success managers, legal, partner managers and marketing.

YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF YOU HAVE:

  • 2 - 4 years of experience in a closing role (esp. in B2B, MarTech, SaaS tech sales)
  • Excellent spoken and written German and English skills, Polish or Czech a plus
  • Hunter mindset: Strong familiarity with outbound prospecting techniques, tools and processes
  • Strong Plus: Experience in working with partners (solution integrators, consultancies, independent software vendors)
  • Multi-tasker who can adapt to changing situations
  • Quick thinker, must be able to handle questions on the fly

NICE TO HAVE:

  • You love tech sales;
  • You have a curious mindset and are capable to understand prospects’ pains and how a deep tech solution like Algolia can solve it;
  • You thrive in dynamic environments and are capable to adjust to constant change;
  • You are excited by the idea of building things and have a structured and strategic approach to working your territory;
  • You believe you are in charge of your own success and you have the grit to continue going when things get challenging.

#LI-Hybrid 

FLEXIBLE WORKPLACE STRATEGY:

Algolia’s flexible workplace model is designed to empower all Algolians to fulfill our mission to power search and discovery with ease. We place an emphasis on an individual’s impact, contribution, and output, over their physical location. Algolia is a high-trust environment and many of our team members have the autonomy to choose where they want to work and when. 

We have a global presence with offices in Paris, NYC, London, Sydney and Bucharest, however we also offer many of our team members the option to work remotely either as fully remote or hybrid-remote employees. Positions listed as "Remote" are only available for remote work within the specified country. Positions listed within a specific city are only available in that location - depending on the role it may be available with either a hybrid-remote or in-office schedule.

WE’RE LOOKING FOR SOMEONE WHO CAN LIVE OUR VALUES:

  • GRIT - Problem-solving and perseverance capability in an ever-changing and growing environment.
  • TRUST - Willingness to trust our co-workers and to take ownership.
  • CANDOR - Ability to receive and give constructive feedback.
  • CARE - Genuine care about other team members, our clients and the decisions we make in the company.
  • HUMILITY - Aptitude for learning from others, putting ego aside.

We’re looking for talented, passionate people to help build the world’s best search and discovery technology. We value autonomy, diversity, and collaboration. We’re committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone is respected and supported—regardless of race, age, ancestry, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, color, veteran status, disability, or socioeconomic background.

IMPORTANT NOTICE FOR CANDIDATES - Recruitment Fraud Notice

We’ve recently seen an increase in recruitment scams targeting job seekers. To help protect yourself, please keep the following in mind:

  • Our open positions may appear on third-party job boards, but the best way to apply safely is directly through our careers page.
  • All genuine communication from Algolia will come from an @algolia.com email address. If you receive an email from someone claiming to work at Algolia who does not have an @algolia.com email address, please do not respond or share any personal information.
  • We’ll never ask for payments, purchases, or financial details during the hiring process.

READY TO APPLY?

If you share our values and our enthusiasm for building the world’s best search & discovery technology, we’d love to review your application!

questions.prompt: How does Algolia handle high-fashion luxury retail?

answer.answer: [WEBSITE STUB] No live algolia.com Ask-AI capture wired yet for: "How does Algolia handle high-fashion luxury retail?". This placeholder exists so the harness runs end-to-end; lab/capture will replace it.

Algolia helps high-fashion and luxury retail brands create the fluid, curated, and high-quality online experiences their customers expect. By leveraging AI, Algolia redefines the search and discovery process to be more intuitive and less dependent on the user's ability to type the perfect query.

### Rethinking the Luxury Search Experience

For luxury fashion, the online shopping experience must be as seamless and curated as an in-store visit. Customers in this segment have high expectations; they anticipate the platform will understand their needs and present them with the right options without extensive effort ([details and examples](https://www.algolia.com/blog/ecommerce/rethinking-search-for-luxury-fashion)).

### The Role of Agentic AI

Algolia utilizes **agentic AI** to transform the search bar into a sophisticated shopping assistant. This approach focuses on execution rather than just responding to prompts. Key aspects include:

*   **Understanding Intent**: The AI is designed to understand the shopper's underlying intent, even from vague or complex queries, and guide them to the desired products.
*   **Curated Results**: It ensures the search results feel like a curated collection, maintaining the brand's high standards and merchandising strategies.
*   **Fluid Journey**: The goal is to make the entire process from discovery to purchase smooth and intuitive, removing friction and enhancing the luxury feel of the digital experience ([details and examples](https://www.algolia.com/blog/ecommerce/rethinking-search-for-luxury-fashion)).

By focusing on these areas, Algolia helps luxury brands build an online presence that upholds their reputation for quality and exceptional service.

> For more support or info, please feel free to browse our [support articles](https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/search) or [docs](https://www.algolia.com/doc). If you still need help, [submit a ticket](https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/requests/new) for customer support.

answer.answer:
Algolia helps high-fashion and luxury retail brands create the sophisticated, personalized, and seamless online shopping experiences their customers expect. By providing relevant and curated digital experiences, Algolia empowers brands to replicate their high-touch in-store service online.

For example, the premium fashion brand **Lacoste** faced challenges with a disjointed search and discovery experience across their digital platforms. According to a [Lacoste customer story](https://www.algolia.com/customer-stories/lacoste/), they used Algolia to unify their search, leading to a more consistent and personalized journey for their shoppers.

Here’s how Algolia addresses the specific needs of luxury retail:

### Curated Merchandising
Luxury brands require precise control over how products are presented. Algolia's merchandising tools allow for this level of curation, enabling brands to:
*   **Strategically rank products** to feature specific designers, new arrivals, or capsule collections.
*   **Create promotional banners** and landing pages for marketing campaigns.
*   **Bundle items** to encourage complete looks and increase average order value.

These tools help brands maintain their specific editorial voice and business goals, as detailed in this guide to [ecommerce merchandising](https://www.algolia.com/blog/ecommerce/ecommerce-merchandising-a-guide-to-the-art-and-science-of-proven-strategies-to-boost-your-sales/).

### Personalization and Optimization
To meet the expectations of luxury shoppers, a personalized experience is key. Lacoste, for instance, used Algolia's Personalization feature to tailor results to individual shoppers. They also leveraged [A/B testing](https://www.algolia.com/doc/guides/ab-testing/what-is-ab-testing/) to experiment with and optimize their search and discovery strategies, ensuring continuous improvement in their conversion rates and user engagement.

By implementing these features, Lacoste was able to create a more relevant and engaging mobile experience, which is crucial as mobile commerce continues to grow.

Is there a particular aspect of the luxury retail experience, such as personalization or merchandising, that you're most interested in exploring?

  • Collaborate across teams to better focus customer-facing efforts

  • Support Algolia’s regional marketing activity (events, conferences, webinars)

  • Multi-tasker who can adapt to changing situations
  • Willingness to speak at events/conferences

We place an emphasis on an individual’s impact, contribution, and output, over their physical location. Algolia is a high-trust environment and many of our team members have the autonomy to choose where they want to work and when. 

We have a global presence with offices in Paris, NYC, London, Sydney and Bucharest, however we also offer many of our team members the option to work remotely either as fully remote or hybrid-remote employees. Positions listed as "Remote" are only available for remote work within the specified country. Positions listed within a specific city are only available in that location - depending on the role it may be available with either a hybrid-remote or in-office schedule.

WE’RE LOOKING FOR SOMEONE WHO CAN LIVE OUR VALUES:

  • GRIT - Problem-solving and perseverance capability in an ever-changing and growing environment.
  • TRUST - Willingness to trust our co-workers and to take ownership.
  • CANDOR - Ability to receive and give constructive feedback.
  • CARE - Genuine care about other team members, our clients and the decisions we make in the company.
  • HUMILITY - Aptitude for learning from others, putting ego aside.

We’re looking for talented, passionate people to help build the world’s best search and discovery technology. We value autonomy, diversity, and collaboration. We’re committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone is respected and supported—regardless of race, age, ancestry, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, color, veteran status, disability, or socioeconomic background.

IMPORTANT NOTICE FOR CANDIDATES - Recruitment Fraud Notice

We’ve recently seen an increase in recruitment scams targeting job seekers. To help protect yourself, please keep the following in mind:

  • Our open positions may appear on third-party job boards, but the best way to apply safely is directly through our careers page.
  • All genuine communication from Algolia will come from an @algolia.com email address. If you receive an email from someone claiming to work at Algolia who does not have an @algolia.com email address, please do not respond or share any personal information.
  • We’ll never ask for payments, purchases, or financial details during the hiring process.

READY TO APPLY?

If you share our values and our enthusiasm for building the world’s best search & discovery technology, we’d love to review your application!

questions.prompt: How does Algolia support fashion ecommerce search?

answer.answer: [WEBSITE STUB] No live algolia.com Ask-AI capture wired yet for: "How does Algolia support fashion ecommerce search?". This placeholder exists so the harness runs end-to-end; lab/capture will replace it.

Algolia supports fashion ecommerce by providing an **Intelligent Fashion Solution** that uses AI to create highly personalized and curated shopping experiences. This solution helps retailers improve user experience, increase conversions, and grow revenue ([details and examples](https://www.algolia.com/about/news/algolia-launches-intelligent-fashion-solution)).

### Intelligent Fashion Solution

Algolia's platform is designed to address the specific needs of online fashion retail. It focuses on transforming both the "art and science" of the shopping experience by delivering AI-powered innovations. This purpose-built solution helps create deeply personal and stylishly curated experiences for modern consumers ([more info](https://www.algolia.com/about/news/algolia-redefines-online-fashion-retail-with-the-next-generation-of-its-intelligent-fashion-solution)).

Key aspects of this solution include:
*   **AI-powered personalization**: Delivers tailored results and recommendations to individual shoppers.
*   **Enhanced Search**: Provides fast, relevant, and intuitive search to help customers find exactly what they're looking for.
*   **Improved User Experience**: Focuses on creating a seamless and engaging customer journey from discovery to checkout.

### Customer Success Example

Fashion retailers like **END. Clothing** have adopted Algolia to enhance their search and recommendation capabilities. By implementing Algolia, the company was able to improve its customer experience, which led to increased conversions, higher click-through rates, and overall revenue growth. This demonstrates the platform's effectiveness in a real-world fashion retail environment ([read the case study](https://www.algolia.com/customers/END.)).

For more support or info, please feel free to browse our [support articles](https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/search) or [docs](https://www.algolia.com/doc). If you still need help, [submit a ticket](https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/requests/new) for customer support.

answer.answer:
Algolia helps fashion e-commerce brands create a fluid and curated shopping experience with AI-powered search and discovery. This allows shoppers to easily find and discover new products, whether they know exactly what they're looking for or just browsing.

Major brands like Gymshark, Under Armour, PUMA, and Lacoste use Algolia to connect their customers with the right products ([Fashion & Apparel](https://www.algolia.com/industries/fashion/)).

### Key Capabilities for Fashion

Here’s how Algolia addresses the specific needs of fashion e-commerce:

*   **Visual and Image Search**
    Shoppers can search using images to "shop the look" and find visually similar items, which is increasingly popular in fashion retail. Algolia also supports color discovery, allowing customers to search for specific colors and get relevant results ([Fashion & Apparel](https://www.algolia.com/industries/fashion/), [UI Design Kit](https://www.algolia.com/doc/guides/building-search-ui/resources/ui-kit/js)).

*   **AI-Powered Ranking and Personalization**
    Algolia's AI Ranking automatically learns from shopper behavior to display the most relevant products first. It also enables personalization to tailor search results and recommendations to individual shoppers, creating a more curated experience ([Fashion & Apparel](https://www.algolia.com/industries/fashion/)).

*   **Advanced Language Understanding**
    Natural Language Understanding and AI-powered synonym detection help decipher complex or broad queries, like "summer dress," ensuring shoppers get relevant results even without using specific keywords ([Fashion & Apparel](https://www.algolia.com/industries/fashion/)).

*   **Omnichannel Experience**
    For brands with both a physical and online presence, Algolia can connect online and in-store inventory to provide a unified search experience for customers ([Fashion & Apparel](https://www.algolia.com/industries/fashion/)).

These features are designed to improve the entire product discovery journey, from initial search to final purchase, ultimately boosting conversion rates and revenue ([The AI Agent Wears Prada](https://www.algolia.com/blog/ecommerce/rethinking-search-for-luxury-fashion)).

Are you currently more focused on improving core search functionality, or are you exploring more advanced AI-driven features like visual search and personalization?

questions.prompt: I suddenly exceeded my record usage — what do I do?

answer.answer: [WEBSITE STUB] No live algolia.com Ask-AI capture wired yet for: "I suddenly exceeded my record usage — what do I do?". This placeholder exists so the harness runs end-to-end; lab/capture will replace it.

If you have exceeded your record usage limit, you will be charged an over-quota fee for the additional records if you are on a paid plan. If you are on a free plan, your service will be blocked until the next billing cycle begins ([details and examples](https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/articles/17245378392977-How-does-Algolia-count-records-and-operations)).

Here’s a breakdown of what to do and how usage is calculated.

### How Algolia Calculates Record Usage

Algolia calculates your record usage based on a **high watermark** for the billing period, not just the current number of records. This provides flexibility for temporary increases in data.

*   **Daily Snapshots**: Algolia takes daily snapshots of your record count.
*   **Highest Days**: At the end of the billing cycle, the four days with the highest record counts are identified.
*   **Flexible Billing**: To avoid charging for temporary spikes, the top three highest days are disregarded. Your final billable usage is based on the **fourth-highest day** of the month ([details and examples](https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/articles/4406552137617-Why-is-my-application-s-Records-usage-different-to-what-I-expected)).
*   **Total Records**: The total record count includes the records in all your indices, including any replicas. For example, an index with 10,000 records and 2 replicas counts as 30,000 records.

### What to Do After Exceeding Your Limit

If you've received a notification or seen a spike in your usage, you should investigate the cause to manage your costs and ensure your implementation is optimized.

1.  **Review Your Indices**: Check your Algolia Dashboard under the "Indices" section to see the current record count for each index. This can help you identify which index is responsible for the increase.
2.  **Investigate for Bugs**: An unexpected increase could be due to a bug in your implementation, such as a runaway loop that is continuously adding data.
3.  **Check for Architectural Issues**: A common reason for high record counts is creating an excessive number of indices (e.g., one index per user or store). It is often more efficient to use a single, larger index and apply filters to show the correct data to each user. This is considered a best practice to avoid performance issues and high costs ([details and examples](https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/articles/26079522826641-Antipatterns)).

### How to Monitor and Control Costs

You can take proactive steps to manage your usage and costs.

*   **Set Up Cost Management**: In your Algolia Dashboard, navigate to the **Billing > Cost Management** tab. Here, you can set a budget trigger to receive a notification if your invoice is projected to exceed a certain limit ([details and examples](https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/articles/11892882592529-How-can-I-monitor-my-application-s-monthly-costs)).
*   **Optimize Your Data**: Ensure you are not indexing unnecessarily large records or creating too many indices where filtering would be a better solution.

For more support or info, please feel free to browse our [support articles](https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/search) or [docs](https://www.algolia.com/doc). If you still need help, [submit a ticket](https://support.algolia.com/hc/en-us/requests/new) for customer support.

sources.text:
How does Algolia count records and operations?
How does Algolia count records?
The number of records equals to the total record count across all your indices, including replicas.
If you exceed the record limit for your paid plan, we charge an over-quota based on the maximum number of records you reach. Your plan may let you pre-purchase discounted, additional records at the beginning of your contract period. If you exceed the record limit for the free plan, you will be blocked until the new billing cycle begins.
To offer flexibility, we ignore the three days when you had the most records. This means we don’t count them in your monthly total record.
For Standard replicas, let’s say you have 5,000 records in main index, and three Standard Replicas for that index. This means you would have a total record count of 5,000 x 4 ( primary index + 3 replicas) = 20,000 records. Your plan may limit the number of records that you can store. As long as you don’t exceed the maximum record count of your plan, you can add, update, or delete as many records as you want.
How does Algolia count indexing operations?
For our latest Build and Grow plans, search requests and records are charged separately, per our pricing page. We don't charge for indexing operations, however, a protective limit of 10,000 indexing operations for every 1000 search requests and records used per month. For more information on indexing operations and limitations, you can follow this article.
For legacy Free, Standard, and Premium PAYG (Pay-As-You-Go) plans, we deduct any search requests performed and records that you store from the usage units included in your plan. A unit is 1,000 search request or 1,000 records. If your usage exceeds the number of units within your plan, we charge you for the additional units at the end of the billing period. We don't charge for indexing operations, however, there is a contractual limit of 10,000 indexing operations for every 1000 search requests and records used per month. For more information on indexing operations and limitations, you can follow this article.
You can opt to commit to a fixed number of units for the year at a discounted rate. For more details, please visit our pricing page.
For older plans, Record, Rule, Synonym, ACL, and Indexing operations are counted against your plan's monthly quota. If the plan's quota is exceeded, you are charged for overages. For more information on indexing operations and limitations, you can follow this article.
Counting search requests
For plans released after July 2020
Every time you make a search request to an Algolia index, or a search request for a multi-index search using multipleQueries, we count one search request.
In Autocomplete for search-as-you-type implementations, a search happens at every keystroke. Each keystroke counts as a search request.
In order to have relevant analytics data, we aggregate the series of keystrokes and count only the latest (final) query made by the end user as a request for analytics purposes.
For example, for a query using search that targets a single index, if a user types the word “apple” (five letters) in the search input, it performs a search for each character ie. "a", "ap", "app", "appl, "apple", resulting in five search requests. This will count however as one search for analytics purposes. 
When searching for “apple” in a multi-index search-as-you-type implementation targeting three indices (without using multipleQueries ie. three separate queries), each keystroke counts as three search requests, resulting in 15 search requests in total. This would count as three searches, one per index, for analytics purposes.
In the previous example, if you used multipleQueries to perform the search targeting three indices, you would only make one request to our server per keystroke, resulting in five search requests.
Calls to the searchForFacetValues method works in the same way as search.
For older plans
Legacy plans calculate search operations in the same way, except that a multi-index search with multipleQueries counts a search operation for every query in index. A search with multipleQueries that targets 3 indices, counts as 3 search requests.
Unused operations
Unused usage units and operations, either included in your plan or purchased additionally, do not roll over to the next service period.
 
Additional Resources:For Indexing Operations: How does Algolia count indexing operations?For Reindexing Operations: How does Algolia count reindexing operations?For Sandbox Applications: How does Algolia count operations for sandbox applications?For Recommend Requests: How is a recommend request calculated against my usage?For InstantSearch Operations: How does Algolia count InstantSearch operations?For Refinement Operations: How does Algolia count refinement operations?For Replica Indices: How does Algolia count operations on replica indices?For Maximum Queries Per Second: How does Algolia calculate the Maximum QPS (Queries Per Second)?

Senior Product Manager, Composition API

At Algolia, we’re proud to be a pioneer and market leader in AI Search, empowering 17,000+ businesses to deliver blazing-fast, predictive search and browse experiences at internet scale. Every week, we power over 30 billion search requests — four times more than Microsoft Bing, Yahoo, Baidu, Yandex, and DuckDuckGo combined.

In 2021, we raised $150 million in Series D funding, quadrupling our valuation to $2.25 billion. This strong foundation enables us to keep investing in our market-leading platform and serving incredible customers like Under Armour, PetSmart, Stripe, Gymshark, and Walgreens.

Algolia helps businesses and developers understand their online users and show them exactly what they need. As a leader in AI search and discovery, we power over 1.8 trillion searches a year – four times more than Microsoft Bing, Yahoo, Baidu, Yandex and DuckDuckGo combined - for more than 18,000 customers from 100+ countries.

The Mission

Your product mission is to enable advanced search and discovery with results from diverse data sources and retrieval methods (e.g. search or generative AI responses). Algolia’s unified approach provides context-aware, dynamic results, meeting today’s user expectations for both diversity and relevance. Your team is building out the next generation of Algolia’s search APIs and the orchestration layer behind it, whilst enabling business users to efficiently curate their results.

The Opportunity

As a Senior Product Manager at Algolia, you will be at the forefront of driving product strategy and execution. This role is critical in shaping our product offerings to meet market demands and exceed our customers' expectations. You will collaborate with cross-functional departments to deliver impactful results and contribute to the overall success of the organisation.

What you'll be doing:

  • Drive product discovery to understand and define who for, why and what we’re building to deliver on the mission.
  • Formulate, agree and communicate the strategy, vision, and roadmap with all key stakeholders.
  • Whether it’s mocking up concepts, writing functional specs or drafting a copy - you’ll do whatever it takes to push your product throughout the end-to-end lifecycle, in collaboration with engineers, designers, other product managers and cross-functional collaborators.
  • Shape delivery processes and foster cross-functional team dynamics to deliver delightful, powerful products that exceed customer expectations and drive tangible business outcomes.
  • Build and nurture an agile and innovative product culture within the team, founded on the importance of continuous, iterative development.
  • Use analytical and quantitative skills to understand usage, inform prioritization and report on progress.
  • Understand the evolving competitive landscape and identify potential strategic partnerships for complementary product offerings.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional partners (legal, marketing, sales, customer success) to successfully launch products, drive adoption, and optimize customer retention.

Role Requirements:

  • B2B SaaS Expertise: Demonstrated success in driving product strategy and delivery in fast-paced B2B SaaS environments, ideally in infrastructure, developer platforms, or complex data products.
  • API Product Leadership:  Proven experience building, launching, and scaling technically sophisticated products, especially those involving APIs, search or data processing.
  • Technical Fluency and Simplification: Ability to deeply understand technical domains, work closely with engineering to solve complex challenges, and translate technical capabilities into clear, customer-centric value propositions.
  • User Empathy Across Personas: Strong user intuition and empathy across a range of personas, from developers and technical architects to business stakeholders. Demonstrated success in collaborating with Product Design and Engineering to deliver intuitive, high-quality experiences.
  • Data-Informed Decision Making: Skilled in leveraging qualitative and quantitative insights to inform product strategy. Comfortable using analytics, experimentation, and customer feedback to measure success, prioritize work, and iterate effectively.
  • End-to-End Product Ownership: A strong track record of shipping impactful products and continuously improving them through agile development practices, cross-functional collaboration, and customer engagement.
  • Cross-Functional Influence and Execution: Exceptional collaboration skills with cross-functional partners - including engineering, design, product marketing, sales, and support - to align teams, drive execution, and bring products successfully to market.
  • Excellent Communication Skills: Outstanding written and verbal communication abilities, with a talent for framing complex ideas clearly for different audiences (executives, internal stakeholders, and customers) alike.

What would make you stand out:

  • Expertise in search & recommendations engines.
  • Experience in both zero-to-one efforts and continuous optimisation at enterprise scale.
  • Technical background, applied to customer-facing industry and business problems.
  • Experience in or good understanding of eCommerce and/or media.
    • If no prior experience: ability and willingness to learn about specific industry trends and ecosystems, most importantly eCommerce.

FLEXIBLE WORKPLACE STRATEGY:

Algolia’s flexible workplace model is designed to empower all Algolians to fulfill our mission to power search and discovery with ease. We place an emphasis on an individual’s impact, contribution, and output, over their physical location. Algolia is a high-trust environment and many of our team members have the autonomy to choose where they want to work and when. 

We have a global presence with offices in Paris, NYC, London, Sydney and Bucharest, however we also offer many of our team members the option to work remotely either as fully remote or hybrid-remote employees. Positions listed as "Remote" are only available for remote work within the specified country. Positions listed within a specific city are only available in that location - depending on the role it may be available with either a hybrid-remote or in-office schedule.

WE’RE LOOKING FOR SOMEONE WHO CAN LIVE OUR VALUES:

  • GRIT - Problem-solving and perseverance capability in an ever-changing and growing environment.
  • TRUST - Willingness to trust our co-workers and to take ownership.
  • CANDOR - Ability to receive and give constructive feedback.
  • CARE - Genuine care about other team members, our clients and the decisions we make in the company.
  • HUMILITY - Aptitude for learning from others, putting ego aside.

We’re looking for talented, passionate people to help build the world’s best search and discovery technology. We value autonomy, diversity, and collaboration. We’re committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone is respected and supported—regardless of race, age, ancestry, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, color, veteran status, disability, or socioeconomic background.

IMPORTANT NOTICE FOR CANDIDATES - Recruitment Fraud Notice

We’ve recently seen an increase in recruitment scams targeting job seekers. To help protect yourself, please keep the following in mind:

  • Our open positions may appear on third-party job boards, but the best way to apply safely is directly through our careers page.
  • All genuine communication from Algolia will come from an @algolia.com email address. If you receive an email from someone claiming to work at Algolia who does not have an @algolia.com email address, please do not respond or share any personal information.
  • We’ll never ask for payments, purchases, or financial details during the hiring process.

READY TO APPLY?

If you share our values and our enthusiasm for building the world’s best search & discovery technology, we’d love to review your application!

  • Expertise in search & recommendations engines, hands-on experience with AI/ML technologies. 
  • Technical background, applied to industry and business problems.
  • Proven experience in designing and implementing workflow automation solutions that empower business users and enhance productivity.
  • Experience in or good understanding of eCommerce and/or media.
    • If no prior experience: ability and willingness to learn about specific industry trends and ecosystems, most importantly eCommerce.

FLEXIBLE WORKPLACE STRATEGY:

Algolia’s flexible workplace model is designed to empower all Algolians to fulfill our mission to power search and discovery with ease. We place an emphasis on an individual’s impact, contribution, and output, over their physical location. Algolia is a high-trust environment and many of our team members have the autonomy to choose where they want to work and when. 

We have a global presence with offices in Paris, NYC, London, Sydney and Bucharest, however we also offer many of our team members the option to work remotely either as fully remote or hybrid-remote employees. Positions listed as "Remote" are only available for remote work within the specified country. Positions listed within a specific city are only available in that location - depending on the role it may be available with either a hybrid-remote or in-office schedule.

WE’RE LOOKING FOR SOMEONE WHO CAN LIVE OUR VALUES:

  • GRIT - Problem-solving and perseverance capability in an ever-changing and growing environment.
  • TRUST - Willingness to trust our co-workers and to take ownership.
  • CANDOR - Ability to receive and give constructive feedback.
  • CARE - Genuine care about other team members, our clients and the decisions we make in the company.
  • HUMILITY - Aptitude for learning from others, putting ego aside.

We’re looking for talented, passionate people to help build the world’s best search and discovery technology. We value autonomy, diversity, and collaboration. We’re committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone is respected and supported—regardless of race, age, ancestry, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, color, veteran status, disability, or socioeconomic background.

IMPORTANT NOTICE FOR CANDIDATES - Recruitment Fraud Notice

We’ve recently seen an increase in recruitment scams targeting job seekers. To help protect yourself, please keep the following in mind:

  • Our open positions may appear on third-party job boards, but the best way to apply safely is directly through our careers page.
  • All genuine communication from Algolia will come from an @algolia.com email address. If you receive an email from someone claiming to work at Algolia who does not have an @algolia.com email address, please do not respond or share any personal information.
  • We’ll never ask for payments, purchases, or financial details during the hiring process.

READY TO APPLY?

If you share our values and our enthusiasm for building the world’s best search & discovery technology, we’d love to review your application!
